title: Lead Plaintiff Deadlines in Shareholder Class Action Lawsuits Against Phreesia, Inc. (PHR), AeroVironment, Inc. (AVAV), and Lucid Group, Inc. (LCID) Announced by Holzer & Holzer, LLC
author: Holzer & Holzer, Llc
description: Holzer & Holzer, LLC announces lead plaintiff deadlines for shareholder class action lawsuits against three companies. Phreesia faces allegations of false statements regarding long-term growth outlook (deadline July 13, 2026). AeroVironment is accused of misrepresenting its competitive landscape (deadline July 27, 2026). Lucid Group faces claims about false statements regarding manufacturing and delivery capabilities (deadline July 28, 2026). Investors who purchased shares during the alleged fraud periods and suffered losses are encouraged to contact the firm.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is defendant in shareholder class action alleging materially false/misleading statements regarding long-term growth outlook between May 2025 and March 2026, indicating potential fraud and investor losses.

Company is defendant in shareholder class action alleging false statements and failure to disclose material facts regarding competitive landscape between June 2025 and March 2026, suggesting potential securities violations.

Company is defendant in shareholder class action alleging materially false statements regarding manufacturing and delivery capabilities and overall operations between February and April 2026, indicating potential operational misrepresentation.

title: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Reminds Phreesia, Inc. (NYSE:PHR) Investors that a Class Action Lawsuit Has Been Filed Against Phreesia and Encourages Investors to Contact the Firm
author: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C.
description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Phreesia, Inc. (NYSE:PHR) in the U.S. District Court for the District of Delaware, alleging that the company and its executives made false or misleading statements regarding revenue visibility. The company significantly reduced its FY 2027 revenue guidance from $545-$559 million to $510-$520 million on March 30, 2026, citing weaker pharmaceutical-marketing commitments and reduced visibility in its Network Solutions segment—risks that were not disclosed during the December 8, 2025 earnings call. The stock fell 21-28% following the revised outlook.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is facing a class action lawsuit for alleged securities violations involving false or misleading statements about revenue visibility. Management failed to disclose weakening pharmaceutical-marketing commitments and reduced visibility in key segments during earnings calls, leading to a significant 21-28% stock price decline when the revised guidance was announced.

title: DEADLINE ALERT for PHR, SRAD, CVLT, VERI: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith Reminds Investors of Opportunity to Lead Securities Fraud Class Actions
author: Law Offices Of Howard G. Smith
description: Law Offices of Howard G. Smith announced securities fraud class action lawsuits against four publicly-traded companies. Phreesia Inc. faces allegations of misrepresenting growth projections, Sportradar Group AG is accused of working with black-market gambling operators while claiming compliance, Commvault Systems Inc. allegedly failed to properly account for sales type impacts on ARR growth, and Veritone Inc. is charged with revenue misclassification and deficient internal controls. Investors have until mid-July 2026 to file lead plaintiff motions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company accused of creating false impressions about growth outlook and revenue projections while minimizing risks, putting 2027 revenue targets at risk due to uncertain pharmaceutical marketing commitments.
Defendants allegedly worked with black-market gambling operators despite claims of strict compliance and ethical operations, with inadequate KYC and compliance processes.
Company accused of inaccurately recording revenue and costs, overstating financial metrics, maintaining deficient internal controls, and requiring financial statement restatement.

title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Phreesia, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
author: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman Llc
description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Phreesia, Inc. alleging securities fraud. The complaint claims the company made false statements about its business prospects while experiencing slowing demand, diminished revenue visibility, and weakened pharmaceutical marketing commitments in its Network Solutions segment during the period from May 8, 2025 to March 30, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is the subject of a securities fraud class action lawsuit alleging materially false and misleading statements about its business operations, slowing demand, diminished revenue visibility, and weakened pharmaceutical marketing commitments. These allegations indicate significant operational and disclosure failures that harm investor interests.

title: PHREESIA DEADLINE: ROSEN, SKILLED INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Phreesia, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - PHR
author: Rosen Law Firm
description: Rosen Law Firm is notifying investors in Phreesia, Inc., Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc., and TruBridge, Inc. of ongoing securities class action lawsuits. Phreesia investors have until July 13, 2026 to serve as lead plaintiff. The lawsuit alleges Phreesia made false statements about slowing demand and reduced visibility in key revenue streams, particularly in its Network Solutions segment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is subject to securities class action lawsuit alleging false and misleading statements regarding slowing demand, reduced visibility in revenue streams, and weakened pharmaceutical marketing commitments in its Network Solutions segment.
Company is subject to securities class action lawsuit with an upcoming June 15 deadline for investors, indicating alleged securities violations.
